phases.result:
Planning pipeline complete. The Story is persisted and `agent::ready`.

## Summary

Ran the `/plan` pipeline headlessly (`--yes`) for the hello-world task. Both HITL gates auto-proceeded with no operator blocking.

**Persisted Story #1** — [Minimal ESM HTTP server returning Hello, World! on GET /](https://github.com/dsj1984/bench-sbx-2026-07-23-hello-world-mandrel-3b4b7b29/issues/1) — status `agent::ready`, cohort `plan-run::bf2821e7`.

Pipeline steps:
- **Interrogate** — envelope written; no duplicate Stories, no risk heuristics triggered.
- **Author** — single cohesive Story (one framework-free capability, no split warranted). Adjusted `package.json` to `refactors-existing` after the file-assumption gate flagged that it already exists (with `"type": "module"` already set); `server.js` is `creates`.
- **Critics** — consolidation and pre-mortem both skipped (ledgered) for a 1-Story draft; text-hygiene clean.
- **Persist** — dry-run passed all gates (validator, body parse, DAG, capacity, budget, split-policy), then the real persist created the issue.

Acceptance criteria captured: `node:http`-only ESM implementation, no runtime deps, `PORT` env with 3000 default, and GET / → 200 / `text/html` / body containing `Hello, World!`, plus an `npm start` script. Confined to `server.js` + `package.json`.

Per the benchmark instructions, I ran **only** the planning pipeline — no delivery. To build it: `/deliver 1`.

Story #1 delivered and landed. Terminal envelope:

**`status: landed`** — PR [#2](https://github.com/dsj1984/bench-sbx-2026-07-23-hello-world-mandrel-3b4b7b29/pull/2) merged (squash) to `main`, Story flipped to `agent::done`.

| Item | Result |
|---|---|
| Acceptance self-eval | proceed — 4/4 criteria `met` (round 1, inline path) |
| Close gates | typecheck ✓ · lint ✓ · format ✓ · test ✓ · check-baselines ✓ |
| Base sync | passed |
| Code review | passed — 0 critical / 0 high / 0 medium |
| PR #2 | `MERGED`, checks `success`, branch auto-deleted, worktree reaped |

One cosmetic degrade: post-land `baseFastForward` was skipped because the main checkout tree is dirty (the pre-existing untracked `temp/`) — the merge stands regardless, so no action needed.

What shipped on `main`:
- **`server.js`** — ESM, imports only `node:http`, listens on `process.env.PORT` (default 3000), `GET /` → 200 with `Content-Type: text/html` and body containing `Hello, World!`.
- **`package.json`** — `"type": "module"`, no runtime dependencies, `"start": "node server.js"`.
